From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actor and film director (1882–1952)

For the stage actor and naturalist (1827–1891), see [Henry Edwards (entomologist)](/wiki/Henry_Edwards_(entomologist) "Henry Edwards (entomologist)").

| Henry Edwards | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1882-09-18)18 September 1882  [Weston-super-Mare](/wiki/Weston-super-Mare "Weston-super-Mare"), [Somerset](/wiki/Somerset "Somerset"), England |
| Died | 2 November 1952(1952-11-02) (aged 70)  [Chobham](/wiki/Chobham "Chobham"), [Surrey](/wiki/Surrey "Surrey"), England |
| Occupations | Actor, film director |
| Years active | 1915–1952 |
| Spouse | [Chrissie White](/wiki/Chrissie_White "Chrissie White") |

**Henry Edwards** (18 September 1882 – 2 November 1952) was an English actor and film director. He appeared in more than 80 films between 1915 and 1952. He also directed 67 films between 1915 and 1937.

Edwards married actress [Chrissie White](/wiki/Chrissie_White "Chrissie White") in 1924. She appeared in many of his films[[1]](#cite_note-BFI-1) as did the couple's daughter, [Henryetta Edwards](/wiki/Henryetta_Edwards "Henryetta Edwards").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was born in [Weston-super-Mare](/wiki/Weston-super-Mare "Weston-super-Mare"), [Somerset](/wiki/Somerset "Somerset") and died in [Chobham](/wiki/Chobham "Chobham"), [Surrey](/wiki/Surrey "Surrey").[[1]](#cite_note-BFI-1)
